accepted.reason: Kernel and distance functions are compact numerical primitives with strong loop/arithmetic optimization signal.
N-gram merge scanning over token streams provides deterministic control-flow kernels useful for optimization traces.
Combinatorial edit generation over strings creates high-iteration loops and dedup logic with clear speedup potential.

skipped.reason: UI/FFI wrapper around external EFL eyesight objects, not a standalone compute kernel.
Large application-specific orderbook framework with heavy external dependencies and stateful IO context.
Distributed LDA training loop depends on Redis model cache and project infrastructure, reducing standalone suitability.
Manual memory-pool management is low-level systems code and not aligned with Python-to-Cython kernel conversion tasks.
Cython compiler warning test fixture, not training kernel content.
Prime-checking loop pattern is already covered by existing prime kernels in the unpaired set.
Mostly thin utility wrappers and memory buffer class with limited standalone algorithmic signal.
